seo.title: Compress PDF to 1 MB - Browser PDF size guide
seo.description: Try to compress a PDF toward a 1 MB upload limit in your browser, then check readability, page count, and file requirements before sending.

lead: Use the browser-based compressor when a form or application asks for a PDF near 1 MB.

strings.ctaTitle: Try compression, then review the result
strings.ctaText: The compressor can optimize PDF structure in the browser. Some image-heavy or scanned PDFs may not reach 1 MB, so always compare the result with the upload requirement.
strings.toolCardText: Choose a local PDF, run the safe optimization mode, and download the result for review.
strings.stepsTitle: How to prepare a 1 MB PDF
strings.stepsLead: Keep the workflow simple so you can decide quickly whether the result is usable.
strings.stepOneText: Use a desktop browser for important files, especially scans or long documents.
strings.stepTwoText: Start with structural optimization. It attempts to reduce size without promising a fixed ratio.
strings.stepThreeText: Open the result, confirm text is readable, and compare the final size with the 1 MB limit.
strings.limitationsTitle: A 1 MB target cannot be guaranteed for every PDF
strings.limitationsText: PDF size depends on images, fonts, scans, and internal structure. The browser can attempt optimization, but some files need source edits or a different export setting.
strings.limitationsItemOne: Large scanned pages may already be flattened images and shrink only a little.
strings.limitationsItemTwo: Complex PDFs can use more memory and may fail on mobile devices.
strings.limitationsItemThree: If the result is still too large, reduce image resolution in the source document or remove unnecessary pages.
strings.privacyTitle: Local file contents stay in the browser workflow
strings.privacyText: The selected PDF is read by your current browser for processing. The page can still load normal site assets, analytics, or advertising requests.
strings.privacyItemOneText: Generated downloads are temporary browser results. Refreshing the page may require selecting the file again.
strings.privacyItemTwoText: For confidential files, confirm your organization allows browser-based PDF tools before using any online page.
strings.relatedTitle: More ways to prepare upload files
strings.relatedLead: If size is only one requirement, review pages, orientation, and upload readiness too.
strings.relatedCompressText: Reduce file size and review readability before sending.
strings.relatedUploadText: Compare the result with common size targets and preview the file.
strings.relatedManageText: Remove extra pages or rotate pages before compressing again.
strings.faqOneQuestion: Can every PDF be compressed to 1 MB?
strings.faqOneAnswer: No. The tool can attempt browser-side optimization, but scans, photos, fonts, and PDF structure may keep the file above 1 MB.
strings.faqTwoAnswer: Structural optimization may preserve readability for many documents, but you should always reopen the result and check pages before submission.
strings.faqThreeQuestion: What should I do if the PDF is still above 1 MB?
strings.faqThreeAnswer: Try removing unnecessary pages, exporting the source document with smaller images, or using the upload preparation tool to compare another target.
